svg.text: The path of a disciple in training to attain wholesomeness
Restrained by the moral code, seeing danger in the slightest fault
Not grasping at features, not enticed by details
Not for pleasure, not for indulgence — only for support of the body
Purifying the mind through all three watches of the night
2. Sense of right and wrong · hirī
Secluded from sensual pleasures (vivicceva kāmehi) · secluded from unwholesome states (vivicca akusalehi dhammehi)
Accompanied by reflection (savitakka) and examination (savicāra) · uplifting joy and pleasure born of seclusion (vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ)
Settling of reflection and examination (vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā)
Internal tranquility (ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ) · unification of mind (cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ)
Free from reflection and examination · joy and pleasure born of collectedness (samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ)
With the fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā)
Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukhaṁ ca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā)
Settling of mental pleasure and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā)
"Just as a hen incubates her eggs properly …
the chicks can break through the shell safely."
Capable of breaking through · capable of awakening · unsurpassed security from bondage
bhabbo abhinibbhidāya · bhabbo sambodhāya · anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya
like a chick from the eggshell
seeing beings pass away and be reborn
“ One accomplished in true knowledge and conduct
is considered the best among gods and humans. ”
